picks.reason: Webb (blended xFIP 3.20 using 40/60 L14/AGG weighting with 16 clean IP in L14) gives SF a decisive starter advantage over Gusto (AGG xFIP 5.48 [sm]); my win estimate for SF is ~63%, vs market vig-removed ~56%, a gap of ~7 pts. picks.reason: Both total prices are flagged stale — the total market is absent per data integrity rules. On the side, Alvarez's AGG xFIP of 3.37 rests on only 12.2 IP, placing it in the "anecdotal" territory of extreme uncertainty; his L3 shows walks (5 BBs in one start) and the L14 is also [sm] at 8.0 IP. Martinez (blended xFIP 4.31, 83 IP AGG) is the more reliable read. With TB at -133 (vig-removed ~56.5%) and my win estimate for TB at ~53–55% given Alvarez's genuine uncertainty, the gap doesn't clear 5 pts.
picks.data_gap: Alvarez's full-season track record — 12.2 total AGG IP is too thin to anchor a bet on either side.

picks.reason: CLE's bullpen is severely depleted — T1 (Cade Smith) and both T2 arms (Gaddis, Armstrong) are all unavailable (0/3), which is my maximum pen-penalty trigger (+0.5 runs against CLE, full-run ERA-equivalent disadvantage). HOU has fresh Hader (T1) and full T2, a significant late-game edge. However Cecconi (AGG xFIP 4.13) vs Teng (AGG xFIP 4.42) is nearly a wash on starters, and HOU's offense faces a superior opponent bullpen despite having the line advantage. My win estimate for HOU is ~58%, market ~56%, gap only ~2 pts. Rain probability at 16% is modest but the roof status is unconfirmed. Below my gate.
picks.data_gap: Minute Maid roof status — would affect park factor (100 base vs 101 closed) and wind adjustment.

picks.reason: May's blended xFIP (40/60 L14/AGG: 3.31) is genuinely elite right now — L3 includes a complete game and two other 6-inning quality starts — and STL's offense (wRC+ 123 vs RHP) is strong vs KC's 92. But at STL -120 (vig-removed ~54.5%), my gap estimate is only ~3–4 pts, below the 5-pt gate. On totals, the 42% rain probability triggers my elevated threshold (+0.3 runs), pushing the minimum from 1.0 to 1.3 runs; my estimated total of ~9.3 (crosswind, 16 mph, +0.1 runs; neutral park) versus the 9.0 line gives a gap of only ~0.3 runs — nowhere near the elevated gate.
picks.data_gap: Rain probability will move; at game time, if rain passes, the total gap might re-qualify. Postponement risk non-trivial at 42% / gusts 26.8 mph.

picks.reason: Cease (AGG xFIP 2.53, Stf+ 108) is among the best starters in baseball; his L3 includes a 7-K, 4-K, and 11-K effort, and his blended number if L14 were not [sm] would only confirm what the AGG already shows. Imanaga (AGG xFIP 3.87, Stf+ 93) is a decent lefty but meaningfully inferior. My win probability for TOR is ~60%, versus market vig-removed ~52.5% on the best price (-117 BetOnline), producing a gap of ~7.5 pts — comfortably above my 5-pt gate. Both T2 arms for TOR are used, but CHC's entire T1 closer committee is also unavailable; the bullpen disadvantages roughly offset. 3 units would require ≥9 pts gap driven by ≥2 independent components — the starter edge is clear but the bullpen component is neutral (both depleted), so only 1 model component drives the edge, capping this at 1 unit per my method. Both total prices are flagged stale — total market absent.
picks.data_gap: TOR T2 depletion is a concern — if Rogers or Hoffman are extended, the pen disadvantage grows; Cease's pitch count and early exit risk are unknown.

More importantly, ATH's T1 bullpen is 0/3 available (all three closer-committee arms used recently), while Sutter Health Park (Runs 121) amplifies late-game bullpen vulnerabilities. Both total prices are flagged stale. Given ceiling constraints and stronger plays available, I pass.

picks.reason: Wheeler (blended xFIP 3.066 using 40/60 L14/AGG weighting with 12 clean L14 IP, Stf+ 106, L3: 7/6/6 IP with 0/1/0 ER) is at peak performance; Peterson (AGG xFIP 3.91 on 33.1 IP, Stf+ 88, no L14 data, L3 of 7/1/6 ER in three consecutive poor starts) is a clear downgrade. PHI's RL -1.5 at +110 implies only 47.6% coverage probability — my estimate is ~58%, producing a gap of ~10.4 pts, clearing both the 5-pt gate and the 9-pt threshold for 3 units. The edge is independently driven by two components: (1) Wheeler's starter superiority is decisive and well-supported by stable data (2) PHI's offense is hot (L10RS 5.6) while Duran (T1 fresh, 1.90 ERA, 38.5% K%) provides a formidable closer advantage vs NYM's T1 Williams (0/1, 25p Thursday). PHI's T2 (Kerkering, Alvarado) is depleted, partially offsetting the bullpen edge, but not enough to reduce my estimate below threshold. All four 3u conditions are met: no starter [sm], no stale prices on the RL market, no movement against my position (ML barely moved), and two independent model components drive the edge. Weather is clean (84°F, 5.4 mph W, 0% rain).
picks.data_gap: Peterson's L14 data is entirely absent — the "no data" flag means his recent trajectory is invisible; any structural improvement would reduce my edge estimate. This is the primary uncertainty.

best_bet.why_this: Wheeler is pitching at an elite level (blended xFIP 3.07) facing a Peterson who has surrendered 14 earned runs in his last 18.1 innings with no L14 data to suggest recovery; the RL at plus-money (+110) means I'm getting paid over even money to back a team I estimate covers ~58% of the time, producing the widest gap on the slate at 10.4 percentage points with two independent model components supporting the edge.
